content.text:
<plugin_info kind="matched_installed">
display_name: Cloudflare
description: Skills for the Cloudflare developer platform: Workers, Durable Objects, Agents SDK, MCP servers, Wrangler CLI, and web performance
skills:
  - agents-sdk: Build AI agents on Cloudflare Workers using the Agents SDK. Load when creating stateful agents, durable workflows, real-time WebSocket apps, scheduled tasks, MCP servers, or chat applications. Covers Agent class, state management, callable RPC, Workflows integration, and React hooks.
  - building-ai-agent-on-cloudflare: Builds AI agents on Cloudflare using the Agents SDK with state management,
real-time WebSockets, scheduled tasks, tool integration, and chat capabilities.
Generates production-ready agent code deployed to Workers.

Use when: user wants to "build an agent", "AI agent", "chat agent", "stateful
agent", mentions "Agents SDK", needs "real-time AI", "WebSocket AI", or asks
about agent "state management", "scheduled tasks", or "tool calling".
  - building-mcp-server-on-cloudflare: Builds remote MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers on Cloudflare Workers
with tools, OAuth authentication, and production deployment. Generates
server code, configures auth providers, and deploys to Workers.

Use when: user wants to "build MCP server", "create MCP tools", "remote
MCP", "deploy MCP", add "OAuth to MCP", or mentions Model Context Protocol
on Cloudflare. Also triggers on "MCP authentication" or "MCP deployment".
  - cloudflare: Comprehensive Cloudflare platform skill covering Workers, Pages, storage (KV, D1, R2), AI (Workers AI, Vectorize, Agents SDK), networking (Tunnel, Spectrum), security (WAF, DDoS), and infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, Pulumi). Use for any Cloudflare development task.
  - durable-objects: Create and review Cloudflare Durable Objects. Use when building stateful coordination (chat rooms, multiplayer games, booking systems), implementing RPC methods, SQLite storage, alarms, WebSockets, or reviewing DO code for best practices. Covers Workers integration, wrangler config, and testing with Vitest.
  - sandbox-sdk: Build sandboxed applications for secure code execution. Load when building AI code execution, code interpreters, CI/CD systems, interactive dev environments, or executing untrusted code. Covers Sandbox SDK lifecycle, commands, files, code interpreter, and preview URLs.
  - web-perf: Analyzes web performance using Chrome DevTools MCP. Measures Core Web Vitals (FCP, LCP, TBT, CLS, Speed Index), identifies render-blocking resources, network dependency chains, layout shifts, caching issues, and accessibility gaps. Use when asked to audit, profile, debug, or optimize page load performance, Lighthouse scores, or site speed.
  - workers-best-practices: Reviews and authors Cloudflare Workers code against production best practices. Load when writing new Workers, reviewing Worker code, configuring wrangler.jsonc, or checking for common Workers anti-patterns (streaming, floating promises, global state, secrets, bindings, observability). Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
  - wrangler: Cloudflare Workers CLI for deploying, developing, and managing Workers, KV, R2, D1, Vectorize, Hyperdrive, Workers AI, Containers, Queues, Workflows, Pipelines, and Secrets Store. Load before running wrangler commands to ensure correct syntax and best practices.
rules:
  - workers: When building applications on Cloudflare Workers or with frameworks that deploy to Cloudflare Workers, strongly prefer retrieval and fetching documentation over training data.
commands:
  - build-agent: Build an AI agent on Cloudflare using the Agents SDK
  - build-mcp: Build a remote MCP server on Cloudflare with tools and OAuth
mcp_servers:
  - cloudflare-docs
  - cloudflare-bindings
  - cloudflare-builds
  - cloudflare-observability
</plugin_info>
<timestamp>Tuesday, May 19, 2026, 3:55 PM (UTC-4)</timestamp>
<user_query>
I want each PR to have its own cloudflare deploy so I can preview the site
</user_query>

I’ll map how this app is built and deployed today, then turn that into a concise plan for per-PR Cloudflare preview deployments without changing anything yet.

**Planning project inspection**

I’m focusing on creating a plan mode to inspect the repo. I think the relevant skills include CI/CD and maybe looking into Cloudflare’s documentation. I need to consider the git workflow as well. 

As I plan, I’ll read the AGENTS and skills while using ReadFile in parallel. I should check if I need to explore any subagents. I want to gather details without making edits, so my approach will solely involve reading the files to understand the project structure, CI, and Cloudflare configuration before beginning updates and tool usage.

input.prompt: Thoroughness: medium. Explore the repository at /Users/duhl/git/anedot-one for existing deployment/build/Cloudflare configuration. Look for wrangler configs, package scripts, framework/build output, static assets, app framework, and deployment docs. Return concise findings with specific file paths and relevant commands/scripts. Do not modify anything.

Thoroughness: medium. Explore the repository at /Users/duhl/git/anedot-one for GitHub Actions or other CI configuration relevant to PR builds/deployments. Identify workflows, triggers, secrets currently used, install/build/test commands, and any existing PR comment/check behavior. Return concise findings with file paths. Do not modify anything.

content.text: I found this repo already deploys to Cloudflare from GitHub Actions, with `web` as a TanStack Start Worker and `api` as a separate Worker. I’m checking the exact scripts/config now so the plan can fit the existing workflow instead of inventing a parallel deploy path.

**Clarifying deployment scope**

I need to clarify the scope of the user's request. They want each pull request to have its own Cloudflare deploy to preview a site, which seems to refer to the web. But I'm wondering if they also want the API to have its own deployment per pull request, as that could be significant. I should also consider the choice between uploading Workers versions for a preview URL versus deploying a named worker for each PR. I plan to look up the official documentation for wrangler regarding versions and previews.

input.search_term: Cloudflare Workers wrangler versions upload preview URLs pull request deployments official docs
input.explanation: Verify the documented Cloudflare mechanism for Workers preview deployments and URL behavior.

input.search_term: Cloudflare Workers GitHub Actions deploy pull request preview deployments official docs
input.explanation: Find official Cloudflare guidance for deploying Workers from GitHub Actions on pull requests.

content.text:
**Checking documentation**

I think I might need to read the README or the documentation to see if they mention anything about previews. It could be important for understanding the features better or maybe it’s just helpful for ensuring I'm not missing something essential. There’s always a chance there’s something useful in those docs that can clarify things further. I just want to make sure I'm covering all my bases before proceeding!

One important scope choice affects the workflow: a web-only preview is mostly a Cloudflare Worker version upload, while a matching API preview needs separate API deployment, secrets, and CORS handling per PR.

questions.prompt: Should each PR preview deploy only the web site, or both the web site and API?

options.label: Web site only, pointed at the existing staging/API config
Web site plus a matching per-PR API preview

content.text:
Since you skipped the scope question, I’ll plan the conservative default: per-PR previews for the `web` site only, using the existing Cloudflare/GitHub Actions deploy path and existing staging/API variables.

**Creating a plan for documentation**

Alright, I’m working on creating a plan that includes the assumption that it's web-only. I should probably mention the documentation sources and ensure I'm not editing previous content. 

I’m considering how to format markdown links with full file paths, like using [`.github/workflows/deploy.yml`](.github/workflows/deploy.yml). The system indicates to use full paths with relative links as examples. I want to keep this plan concise, and it might be useful to include some existing script snippets and todos.

input.name: PR Cloudflare Previews
input.overview: Add a GitHub Actions pull-request preview deployment for the `web` Worker so every PR gets a stable Cloudflare preview URL. This will reuse the existing pnpm/Turbo/Wrangler setup and avoid changing production or staging deploy behavior.
input.plan:
# PR Cloudflare Preview Deployments

## Assumptions
- Scope is the `web` site only, not a separate per-PR `api` Worker.
- Preview builds should use the existing GitHub secrets/vars already used by deploys: `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN`, `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID`, `VITE_WORKOS_CLIENT_ID`, `VITE_WORKOS_API_HOSTNAME`, and `SERVER_URL`.
- The stable PR URL should be an aliased Cloudflare Worker preview URL such as `pr-123-web.<account>.workers.dev` format as generated by Cloudflare, not a custom domain.

## Existing Context
- [`package.json`](package.json) already has `deploy:web:preview` as `pnpm build:web && pnpm --filter web deploy:preview`.
- [`apps/web/package.json`](apps/web/package.json) already maps `deploy:preview` to `wrangler versions upload`.
- [`pnpm-workspace.yaml`](pnpm-workspace.yaml) pins `wrangler` at `^4.93.0`, which supports aliased preview URLs.
- [`apps/web/wrangler.jsonc`](apps/web/wrangler.jsonc) defines the `web` Worker and staging/production names.
- [`README.md`](README.md) already documents non-production branches using `npx wrangler versions upload`.
- [`github/workflows/deploy.yml`](.github/workflows/deploy.yml) deploys production/staging on branch pushes and should remain unchanged for mainline deploys.

Relevant existing command:

```json
"deploy:web:preview": "pnpm build:web && pnpm --filter web deploy:preview"
```

## Proposed Approach
Add a new PR-only GitHub Actions workflow, likely [`github/workflows/preview.yml`](.github/workflows/preview.yml), rather than mixing preview deployment into the existing branch deploy workflow. The workflow will:

1. Trigger on `pull_request` events for opened, synchronized, reopened, and ready-for-review PRs.
2. Skip draft PRs unless explicitly ready for review.
3. Install pnpm `10.20.0` and Node `25.3.0`, matching existing CI and deploy workflows.
4. Run `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` and `pnpm check`.
5. Build `web` with `pnpm --filter web build`.
6. Upload a Cloudflare Worker preview version using the generated config:
   - `pnpm --filter web exec wrangler versions upload --config dist/server/wrangler.json --preview-alias "pr-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"`
7. Capture the preview URL from Wrangler output and post or update a PR comment with the URL.

## GitHub Permissions and Secrets
The preview workflow should request only what it needs:

- `contents: read` to check out the repo.
- `pull-requests: write` or `issues: write` to create/update the PR comment.

It will use the same Cloudflare secrets as [`github/workflows/deploy.yml`](.github/workflows/deploy.yml). If the repo blocks secrets on forked PRs, the workflow should only deploy for trusted PR contexts and let CI still run for forks.

## Cloudflare Behavior
This follows Cloudflare's documented Worker Preview URL flow:

- `wrangler versions upload` creates a Worker version and returns a preview URL.
- `--preview-alias` creates a stable human-readable alias for that version.
- Preview URLs are public when enabled; Cloudflare Access can be added later if previews need authentication.

Sources:
- https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/previews/
- https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/ci-cd/external-cicd/github-actions/index.md

## Verification
- Open or update a test PR and confirm the `Preview` workflow runs.
- Confirm the workflow posts one updated PR comment with the Cloudflare preview URL.
- Push another commit to the same PR and confirm the `pr-N` alias updates to the newest build.
- Confirm `Deploy` still only runs on `master` and `production` pushes.
- Confirm preview deployment does not require production secrets beyond the existing Cloudflare deploy token and build-time public/env vars.

todos.content: Create a PR-only GitHub Actions preview workflow using the existing pnpm, Node, check, and web build steps.
Use `wrangler versions upload` with a `pr-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}` preview alias against `dist/server/wrangler.json`.
Capture the Wrangler preview URL and create or update a single PR comment with the preview link.
Verify the workflow on a PR and confirm staging/production deploy workflow behavior is unchanged.
